OpenInstead
Home Categories Articles

Open source alternatives
to every SaaS you pay for.

Browse 68 popular cloud tools and see which self-hosted, free or open source projects can replace them. No fluff, no affiliate spam — just side-by-side facts.

68 SaaS covered 99 open source projects 185 direct comparisons

Browse by category

Every tool is grouped by what it does. Pick the category, see the incumbents, and jump straight to open source replacements.

📒 Note-taking & Knowledge Base

Tools for personal notes, wikis, and knowledge management — replacing Notion, Evernote, and friends with self-hosted or open source alternatives.

3 SaaS 5 alternatives

💬 Team Communication

Chat, messaging and voice platforms for teams — open source replacements for Slack, Discord, and WhatsApp.

4 SaaS 5 alternatives

🎥 Video Conferencing

Meetings, webinars and virtual classrooms — self-hosted alternatives to Zoom, Google Meet and Microsoft Teams.

3 SaaS 2 alternatives

📋 Project Management & Kanban

Task boards, sprints, roadmaps and issue tracking — open source alternatives to Asana, Jira, Trello and Monday.

7 SaaS 8 alternatives

Cloud drive and file synchronization — replace Dropbox, Google Drive, and OneDrive with self-hosted options.

Collaborative document, spreadsheet, and presentation editors — open source alternatives to Google Docs and Microsoft 365.

Graphic design, image editing, and vector tools — open source alternatives to Figma, Canva, Photoshop and Illustrator.

Connect apps and automate repetitive tasks — self-hosted alternatives to Zapier, IFTTT and Make.

Why open source alternatives?

Every SaaS you pay for is a monthly bill, a data-sharing decision and a vendor lock-in risk. Open source alternatives give you ownership: install on your own server, run forever without a subscription, and keep your data inside your walls. Some are drop-in; others ask you to learn new habits. We tell you which is which.

OpenInstead — open source alternatives to the SaaS you already use. Updated April 2026.
Disclaimer: licensing, features and pricing change. Verify each tool's current status on its official site before committing.
